title: FIT — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Scope Feature × Market Kano + 9-ODI Fit
sections.content:
Scope: 6 technology-scoped features from Step 01 (Feat. #1–5, #8) × single target market (NAICS 221115). Features #6, #7, #9 are vendor-scoped → Step 11.

### 09a — Scores

| # | Feature | Kano | T | Co | Sa | Re | Sk | St | Pa | Cf | De | **Fit** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | must_be | 5 | 6 | 6 | 7 | 4 | 5 | 4 | 7 | 4 | **5.33** |
| F2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | one_dimensional | 6 | 5 | 7 | 8 | 4 | 5 | 4 | 8 | 5 | **5.78** |
| F3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | must_be | 5 | 5 | 8 | 8 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 8 | 3 | **5.44** |
| F4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | one_dimensional | 7 | 9 | 4 | 6 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | **6.56** |
| F5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | must_be | 9 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 7 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 5 | **7.78** |
| F8 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | must_be | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 5 | 6 | 7 | 6 | 4 | **5.78** |

T=Time · Co=Cost · Sa=Safety · Re=Reliability · Sk=Skill · St=Stress · Pa=Pain · Cf=Confidence · De=Delight. Scale 1–10. `overall_fit` = arithmetic mean, rounded to 2dp.

### Rationale (per feature)

| # | Kano reasoning | Primary drivers | Market evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Every BESS EPC delivers a containerized asset — commodity-FP baseline. Absence = not an EPC offering. | Reliability (LFP cycle + thermal mgmt), Confidence (UL 9540A / IEC 62619). | N10 firmware incompat., N16 capacity fade detection, N19 cycling-mgmt. |
| F2 | PCS performance is measurable and linear: response time, Q capability, fault ride-through. More is better. | Reliability, Safety (fault ride-through), Confidence. | N13 aFRR/FCR <30 s, N15 VDE-AR-N 4110 FRT. |
| F3 | Without MV coupling the asset cannot energize. Assumed; not a delighter. | Safety (protection), Reliability, Confidence. | N4 MV-capacity blocker, N15 grid-fault ride-through. |
| F4 | Linear — richer dispatch optimization → more revenue captured. Drives the business case. | Cost (revenue stack), Pain (manual dispatch), Stress. | N2 bankable revenue model, N14 >90% curtailment capture, N18 re-tune effort. |
| F5 | Without FGW TR8 + VDE-AR-N 4110 the asset cannot legally inject. Pure must_be — but the highest-`overall_fit` because every stakeholder pain (schedule, re-cert, inertia-prequal) runs through certification. | Time (critical-path), Safety (regulatory), Confidence, Stress. | N6 <9 mo RtB, N9 TR8 re-submission, N11 witness-test NCs, N12 grid-forming cert gap, N20 annual re-cert. |
| F8 | Civil is table-stakes. No one wins a wind IPP on Baugenehmigung quality. | Pain (Feuerwehr redesign), Time, Cost. | N5 footprint in fence, N7 BoP variance, N8 fire-clearance redesign. |

### 09b — Validation checks

| Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Kano cross-market consistency | PASS | Single-market run; no cross-market contradiction. |
| Safety ≥7 ⇒ Confidence ≥4 | PASS | F3 Sa=8/Cf=8; F5 Sa=9/Cf=9. |
| Skill ≥7 ⇒ Stress ≥4 | PASS | F5 Sk=7/St=8. |
| No feature scores 10 on all dims | PASS | Max dim = 9 (F5). |
| overall_fit arithmetic | PASS | Recomputed in-cell. |
| Feature names (spec-visible, 2–6 words, no brand/superlative) | PASS | |
| Accuracy/Reliability feature scoring 1 on Reliability | PASS | No such case. |
| Safety interlock scoring 1 on Safety | PASS | No such case. |

### Summary — what fits, what doesn't (market 221115)

| Band | Features | Read |
|---|---|---|
| Strong fit (≥7) | F5 Grid-Code Cert | The single most leveraged feature in this market — Jan 2026 inertia-service prequal + 6–12 mo cert lead sit on the critical path. |
| Moderate (5.5–7) | F4 EMS, F2 PCS, F8 Civil | Revenue-stack optimizer (F4) is the bankability lever; PCS (F2) owns the ancillary-services response envelope. |
| Baseline (≤5.5) | F1 BESS, F3 MV | Table-stakes must_be — no points for having them, all downside for botching them. |

**No `reverse` classifications.** **No `indifferent` classifications** — every technology-scope feature is needed at this market's commodity-FP baseline.

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that exist *because* Mein Solar chose containerized Li-ion BESS + EPC integration. Each would disappear under a different mechanism (flow, flywheel, P2X). Subset of market 221115's affected list — full list applies across the multi-market surviving set.

### 10a — Generated needs (from Step 07 constraints)

| ID | Source constraint | Statement (error-first) | Imp | Sat | Affected markets (this wf scope) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN1 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance redesign triggered by Li-ion deflagration-venting and compartmentation rules that do not apply to non-flammable storage chemistries | 8 | 4 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 · Other EPG 221118 |
| PRN2 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | Minimize the likelihood of insurance-premium step-up or coverage denial driven by Li-ion fire-loss loss-history that flow/flywheel/CAES assets avoid | 7 | 3 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 |
| PRN3 | Land Footprint and Siting | Increase the ability to fit the storage asset inside an existing wind-park fenced footprint at 40–80 m²/MWh without a new Bebauungsplan change — a footprint a flywheel-farm or standalone condenser would not require | 8 | 5 | Wind 221115 |
| PRN4 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | Minimize the likelihood of procurement disruption from LFP-cell CN-supplier concentration, FX swings, CRMA/EU Battery Regulation change — exposure absent for mechanical and thermal storage | 7 | 4 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 · Other EPG 221118 |
| PRN5 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | Minimize the likelihood of derating or cycle-life loss when exposed ambient drifts below −20 °C or above +50 °C — a constraint flow-battery stacks and CAES tolerate differently | 6 | 5 | Wind 221115 |
| PRN6 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | Minimize the likelihood of a revenue shortfall from 10–15% roundtrip-loss floor eating intraday-arbitrage and curtailment-capture margin — worse than a pumped-hydro round-trip at comparable scale | 7 | 5 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 |
| PRN7 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | Minimize the likelihood of business-case failure when installed cost exceeds €250–400/kWh — a price envelope a thermal-storage or hybrid-H2 option clears differently | 7 | 5 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 · Other EPG 221118 |
| PRN8 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | Minimize the likelihood that FGW TR8 + TR3 grid-forming-mode certification gaps disqualify the asset from the Jan-2026 TSO inertia-service product — a mechanism-specific gap a synchronous condenser would not face | 9 | 3 | Wind 221115 · Solar 221114 |
| PRN9 | Round-Trip Energy Losses (electrochemical) | Minimize the likelihood of warranty-throughput breach driven by aging-aware-cycling misconfiguration on LFP cells — a failure mode electromechanical stores (flywheel) and mechanical stores (CAES) do not share | 8 | 5 | Wind 221115 |
| PRN10 | MV Grid Connection Capacity (BESS siting locus) | Minimize the likelihood of DSO rejection when the BESS is added to a pre-energized wind MV cabinet without revised protection-coordination study — driven by BESS bidirectional PCS behaviour alien to generator-only wind connections | 9 | 4 | Wind 221115 |

### 10b — Validation

| Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Litmus test (would disappear under different tech) | PASS | Each traces to a chemistry/mechanism-specific failure mode (Li-ion fire, LFP supply, PCS bidirectional behaviour, electrochemical cycle fade). Generic "accurate sizing" needs excluded — those are market-level (see N1/N2/N14). |
| Source-constraint traceability | PASS | Every `source_constraint` exists in Step 07; causal link verified. |
| Affected-markets plausibility | PASS | Wind-specific (PRN3 fenced footprint, PRN10 DSO protection) narrowed; cross-market needs (PRN1/2/4/7/8) cover Solar/OEG where constraint also manifests. |
| Completeness | PASS | 9/9 Step-07 constraints covered (Li-ion hazard generates 2 needs: fire-clearance + insurance). Grid-code lead time produces PRN8 (grid-forming-mode). Round-trip losses produce PRN6 (revenue) and PRN9 (warranty). |
| Importance<3 AND Sat>7 (meaningless) | PASS | None. |
| Not duplicating Step-06 market needs | PASS | N5/N8/N10/N12/N16/N17/N19/N21 carried `product_related=true` in JTBD; PRN1–10 extend via mechanism-specific causal text not in Step-06 statements. |

### 10c — Neo4j write preview

```
(Constraint {name:"Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard", run_id})
   -[:generates_need]-> (ErrorStatement {product_related:true}) x2  (PRN1, PRN2)

(NeutralProductGroup {name:"Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC"})
   -[:has_product_need]-> (ErrorStatement) x10

FOR EACH affected_market NAICS ∈ {221115, 221114, 221118}:
   (ErrorStatement)-[:applies_to_market]->(Market {naics_code, run_id})
```

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature × Market (Mein Solar vs. Best Incumbent)
sections.content:
Scope: 3 vendor-scope features (F6, F7, F9) + 2 technology-scope features where Mein Solar's implementation diverges meaningfully from incumbent baseline (F4 EMS, F5 Grid-Code Cert). Incumbents per JTBD anchor set: Fluence, Tesla Megapack (turnkey), Wärtsilä (GEMS), Siemens Gamesa, utility in-house EPC.

### 11a — Comparison table (market 221115)

| # | Feature | Kano | Ven | Inc | Best incumbent | Adv |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| V1 | Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid Design (#6, vendor) | attractive | 8 | 5 | Utility in-house EPC (EnBW, RWE) | **superior** |
| V2 | EV-Charging Buffer Configuration (#7, vendor) | indifferent | 6 | 3 | — (not relevant to wind IPP) | parity* |
| V3 | Single-Source EPC Contract Vehicle (#9, vendor) | attractive | 8 | 5 | Fluence turnkey | **superior** |
| V4 | Energy Management System (#4, tech — vendor impl.) | one_dimensional | 5 | 8 | Wärtsilä GEMS | **inferior** |
| V5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery (#5, tech — vendor impl.) | must_be | 7 | 8 | Fluence / Siemens Gamesa | parity |

\* V2 is `indifferent` in this market (wind IPP has no HPC load) → advantage is effectively immaterial; downgraded to `parity` per Kano-market-appropriateness rule.

### Rationale (feature-level, with spec comparison)

| # | Rationale |
|---|---|
| V1 | Mein Solar carries PV-EPC design authority into BESS sizing — a joint PV+BESS DC-coupled or AC-coupled system under one grid-code filing. Utility in-house EPCs split the PV and BESS scope across separate GCs and separate TR8 dossiers. Attractive for wind IPPs with *existing* on-site PV or those running PPA-firming scenarios. Not delight-grade for pure wind (no PV on site) — but still superior for the growing PV-on-wind-site retrofit path. Vendor 8 / incumbent 5 = **superior** (gap >1). |
| V2 | Sizing BESS as an HPC buffer is a relevant capability in retail-forecourt and logistics markets (NAICS 457120 / 488190). For a wind IPP there is no HPC load at the point of connection. The feature is Kano-`indifferent` in this market → both scores bounded low; advantage reads `parity` not `superior`. |
| V3 | Single EPC counterparty owning battery + PCS + MV + EMS + civil + warranty wrap. Fluence turnkey is the closest incumbent but delivers as product-with-install-services — subcontracts civil and MV locally, leaving interface risk on the IPP. Wärtsilä / Siemens Gamesa similar. Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency = genuine single-counterparty delivery, directly answering N24 (cross-vendor dispute). Attractive. Vendor 8 / incumbent 5 = **superior**. |
| V4 | EMS is where Mein Solar is *behind* the stack. Wärtsilä GEMS + Fluence Mosaic offer dispatch-optimization with curtailment, arbitrage, FCR/aFRR, inertia, and aggregator-API integration productized and battle-tested at utility scale. Mein Solar does not have a proprietary EMS and will integrate a third-party EMS (Next Kraftwerke / Entrix / ampere.cloud) — a valid stance but the *system fit* on V4 is inferior until a partnership is formalized. Directly exposed on N2/N14/N18. Vendor 5 / incumbent 8 = **inferior**. |
| V5 | FGW TR8 + VDE-AR-N 4110 cert path is a Mein Solar strength (PV-EPC heritage), but grid-forming-mode (TR3) prequal for Jan-2026 inertia service is an emerging capability every incumbent is racing to prove. Fluence / Siemens Gamesa have public grid-forming reference sites; Mein Solar's reference is grid-following at 4.5 MW / 9 MWh. Parity until Mein Solar demonstrates grid-forming mode on a certified plant. |

### 11b — Objectivity review

| Check | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Vendor-bias distribution (>70% superior) | PASS | 2 superior / 2 parity / 1 inferior. |
| Anti-vendor bias (>70% inferior) | PASS | |
| Advantage arithmetic (gap vs. label) | PASS | V1 +3 sup · V2 parity · V3 +3 sup · V4 −3 inf · V5 −1 parity. |
| Vendor ≥9 consistently (marketing inflation) | PASS | Max vendor = 8. |
| Incumbent ≤3 consistently (straw-man) | PASS | Min incumbent = 3 (V2, which is rated `indifferent`). |
| Kano fit: must_be both ≥5 | PASS | V5 7/8. |
| Kano fit: attractive vendor>incumbent | PASS | V1, V3. |
| Rationale references concrete specs | PASS | See rationale column (grid-forming mode, aggregator-API EMS, joint PV+BESS cert). |
| Feature names (generic, no brand) | PASS | "Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid Design" not "Mein Solar PV+BESS Package". |

### 11c — Neo4j write preview

```
MERGE (f:Feature {name, run_id}) SET f.feature_scope = "vendor"
MERGE (p:Product {name:"EPC für Großbatteriespeicher",
                  company_name:"Mein Solar GmbH", run_id})
     -[:has_feature]-> (f)
MERGE (f)-[:kano_for_market {
   kano_class, vendor_score, best_incumbent_score,
   best_incumbent_technology, advantage,
   feature_scope:"vendor", rationale
}]-> (m:Market {naics_code:"221115", run_id})
```

---

sections.title: Strategic read
sections.content:
| Lever | Signal | Action implication |
|---|---|---|
| **Grid-Code + Cert (F5)** | Highest overall_fit (7.78). Runs through N6/N9/N11/N12/N20. | Treat cert as the product. Sell the dossier + DSO-acceptance schedule certainty, not MW/MWh. |
| **EMS gap (V4)** | Inferior to Wärtsilä/Fluence. Exposes N2/N14/N18. | Either white-label a Tier-1 EMS with a named partnership (Next Kraftwerke / Entrix / Flexpower) **or** OEM a dispatch-optimizer with aggregator API — block the "we hand you the EMS problem" reading. |
| **Grid-forming certification (PRN8 + V5)** | Jan-2026 TSO inertia-service window opens and closes fast. Parity today, could be inferior by mid-2026. | Commit a grid-forming PCS reference + TR3 cert on the next Wind+BESS project; this is the highest-opportunity technical bet. |
| **PV+BESS hybrid (V1)** | Attractive today. | Sell this into *repowering* (S2) and *greenfield new-build with on-site PV* (S1) first — these are the segments where the differentiator is mechanically activated. |
| **Single-source EPC wrap (V3)** | Attractive, and N24's emotional anchor. | Requires warranty-wrap documentation + named RCA process. Marketing without warranty-wrap = parity, not superior. |
| **Fire-clearance redesign (PRN1)** | Product-related, importance 8 / satisfaction 4. | Standardize fire-clearance templates per Feuerwehr region — turns a recurring N8/PRN1 slip into a priced delta on the EPC contract. |

---

rows.Kano reasoning: Every BESS EPC delivers a containerized asset — commodity-FP baseline. Absence = not an EPC offering.
rows.Primary drivers: Reliability (LFP cycle + thermal mgmt), Confidence (UL 9540A / IEC 62619).
rows.Market evidence: N10 firmware incompat., N16 capacity fade detection, N19 cycling-mgmt.

rows.Kano reasoning: PCS performance is measurable and linear: response time, Q capability, fault ride-through. More is better.
rows.Market evidence: N13 aFRR/FCR <30 s, N15 VDE-AR-N 4110 FRT.

rows.Kano reasoning: Without MV coupling the asset cannot energize. Assumed; not a delighter.
rows.Market evidence: N4 MV-capacity blocker, N15 grid-fault ride-through.

rows.Kano reasoning: Linear — richer dispatch optimization → more revenue captured. Drives the business case.
rows.Market evidence: N2 bankable revenue model, N14 >90% curtailment capture, N18 re-tune effort.

rows.Kano reasoning: Without FGW TR8 + VDE-AR-N 4110 the asset cannot legally inject. Pure must_be — but the highest-`overall_fit` because every stakeholder pain (schedule, re-cert, inertia-prequal) runs through certification.
rows.Market evidence: N6 <9 mo RtB, N9 TR8 re-submission, N11 witness-test NCs, N12 grid-forming cert gap, N20 annual re-cert.

rows.Kano reasoning: Civil is table-stakes. No one wins a wind IPP on Baugenehmigung quality.
rows.Market evidence: N5 footprint in fence, N7 BoP variance, N8 fire-clearance redesign.

rows.Check: No feature scores 10 on all dims

rows.Read: The single most leveraged feature in this market — Jan 2026 inertia-service prequal + 6–12 mo cert lead sit on the critical path.

rows.Features: F4 EMS, F2 PCS, F8 Civil
rows.Read: Revenue-stack optimizer (F4) is the bankability lever; PCS (F2) owns the ancillary-services response envelope.
Table-stakes must_be — no points for having them, all downside for botching them.

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr / VdS fire-clearance redesign triggered by Li-ion deflagration-venting and compartmentation rules that do not apply to non-flammable storage chemistries
Minimize the likelihood of insurance-premium step-up or coverage denial driven by Li-ion fire-loss loss-history that flow/flywheel/CAES assets avoid

Increase the ability to fit the storage asset inside an existing wind-park fenced footprint at 40–80 m²/MWh without a new Bebauungsplan change — a footprint a flywheel-farm or standalone condenser would not require
Minimize the likelihood of procurement disruption from LFP-cell CN-supplier concentration, FX swings, CRMA/EU Battery Regulation change — exposure absent for mechanical and thermal storage
Minimize the likelihood of derating or cycle-life loss when exposed ambient drifts below −20 °C or above +50 °C — a constraint flow-battery stacks and CAES tolerate differently
Minimize the likelihood of a revenue shortfall from 10–15% roundtrip-loss floor eating intraday-arbitrage and curtailment-capture margin — worse than a pumped-hydro round-trip at comparable scale
Minimize the likelihood of business-case failure when installed cost exceeds €250–400/kWh — a price envelope a thermal-storage or hybrid-H2 option clears differently

Minimize the likelihood that FGW TR8 + TR3 grid-forming-mode certification gaps disqualify the asset from the Jan-2026 TSO inertia-service product — a mechanism-specific gap a synchronous condenser would not face

Minimize the likelihood of warranty-throughput breach driven by aging-aware-cycling misconfiguration on LFP cells — a failure mode electromechanical stores (flywheel) and mechanical stores (CAES) do not share

rows.Source constraint: MV Grid Connection Capacity (BESS siting locus)
rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of DSO rejection when the BESS is added to a pre-energized wind MV cabinet without revised protection-coordination study — driven by BESS bidirectional PCS behaviour alien to generator-only wind connections

rows.Check: Litmus test (would disappear under different tech)
rows.Note: Each traces to a chemistry/mechanism-specific failure mode (Li-ion fire, LFP supply, PCS bidirectional behaviour, electrochemical cycle fade). Generic "accurate sizing" needs excluded — those are market-level (see N1/N2/N14).
Every `source_constraint` exists in Step 07; causal link verified.
Wind-specific (PRN3 fenced footprint, PRN10 DSO protection) narrowed; cross-market needs (PRN1/2/4/7/8) cover Solar/OEG where constraint also manifests.

9/9 Step-07 constraints covered (Li-ion hazard generates 2 needs: fire-clearance + insurance). Grid-code lead time produces PRN8 (grid-forming-mode). Round-trip losses produce PRN6 (revenue) and PRN9 (warranty).
N5/N8/N10/N12/N16/N17/N19/N21 carried `product_related=true` in JTBD; PRN1–10 extend via mechanism-specific causal text not in Step-06 statements.

rows.Feature: Energy Management System (#4, tech — vendor impl.)
Grid-Code Certification Delivery (#5, tech — vendor impl.)

rows.Rationale: Mein Solar carries PV-EPC design authority into BESS sizing — a joint PV+BESS DC-coupled or AC-coupled system under one grid-code filing. Utility in-house EPCs split the PV and BESS scope across separate GCs and separate TR8 dossiers. Attractive for wind IPPs with *existing* on-site PV or those running PPA-firming scenarios. Not delight-grade for pure wind (no PV on site) — but still superior for the growing PV-on-wind-site retrofit path. Vendor 8 / incumbent 5 = **superior** (gap >1).

Sizing BESS as an HPC buffer is a relevant capability in retail-forecourt and logistics markets (NAICS 457120 / 488190). For a wind IPP there is no HPC load at the point of connection. The feature is Kano-`indifferent` in this market → both scores bounded low; advantage reads `parity` not `superior`.

Single EPC counterparty owning battery + PCS + MV + EMS + civil + warranty wrap. Fluence turnkey is the closest incumbent but delivers as product-with-install-services — subcontracts civil and MV locally, leaving interface risk on the IPP. Wärtsilä / Siemens Gamesa similar. Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency = genuine single-counterparty delivery, directly answering N24 (cross-vendor dispute). Attractive. Vendor 8 / incumbent 5 = **superior**.

EMS is where Mein Solar is *behind* the stack. Wärtsilä GEMS + Fluence Mosaic offer dispatch-optimization with curtailment, arbitrage, FCR/aFRR, inertia, and aggregator-API integration productized and battle-tested at utility scale. Mein Solar does not have a proprietary EMS and will integrate a third-party EMS (Next Kraftwerke / Entrix / ampere.cloud) — a valid stance but the *system fit* on V4 is inferior until a partnership is formalized. Directly exposed on N2/N14/N18. Vendor 5 / incumbent 8 = **inferior**.

FGW TR8 + VDE-AR-N 4110 cert path is a Mein Solar strength (PV-EPC heritage), but grid-forming-mode (TR3) prequal for Jan-2026 inertia service is an emerging capability every incumbent is racing to prove. Fluence / Siemens Gamesa have public grid-forming reference sites; Mein Solar's reference is grid-following at 4.5 MW / 9 MWh. Parity until Mein Solar demonstrates grid-forming mode on a certified plant.

rows.Note: V1 +3 sup · V2 parity · V3 +3 sup · V4 −3 inf · V5 −1 parity.
See rationale column (grid-forming mode, aggregator-API EMS, joint PV+BESS cert).
"Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid Design" not "Mein Solar PV+BESS Package".

rows.Action implication: Treat cert as the product. Sell the dossier + DSO-acceptance schedule certainty, not MW/MWh.
Either white-label a Tier-1 EMS with a named partnership (Next Kraftwerke / Entrix / Flexpower) **or** OEM a dispatch-optimizer with aggregator API — block the "we hand you the EMS problem" reading.

rows.Signal: Jan-2026 TSO inertia-service window opens and closes fast. Parity today, could be inferior by mid-2026.
rows.Action implication: Commit a grid-forming PCS reference + TR3 cert on the next Wind+BESS project; this is the highest-opportunity technical bet.
Sell this into *repowering* (S2) and *greenfield new-build with on-site PV* (S1) first — these are the segments where the differentiator is mechanically activated.
Requires warranty-wrap documentation + named RCA process. Marketing without warranty-wrap = parity, not superior.
Standardize fire-clearance templates per Feuerwehr region — turns a recurring N8/PRN1 slip into a priced delta on the EPC contract.

entries.rationale: Every BESS EPC delivers a containerized asset under commodity FP; must_be baseline. Primary drivers: Reliability (7) from LFP/BMS/thermal design, Confidence (7) from UL 9540A/IEC 62619 lineage. Evidence: N10 firmware incompat., N16 fade detection, N19 cycling management.

Response time, Q-capability, and grid-fault ride-through scale linearly with PCS sophistication. Primary drivers: Reliability (8), Safety (7) via FRT, Confidence (8). Evidence: N13 aFRR/FCR <30 s, N15 VDE-AR-N 4110 FRT.
Without MV coupling the asset cannot energize. Primary drivers: Safety (8) via protection relays, Reliability (8), Confidence (8). Evidence: N4 MV-capacity blocker, N15 grid-fault ride-through.

Richer dispatch optimization = more revenue captured. Primary drivers: Cost (9) revenue-stack, Pain (7) removes manual dispatch, Stress (7). Evidence: N2 bankable revenue model, N14 >90% curtailment capture, N18 re-tune effort.

Must_be legally (no cert → no injection) but the highest-leverage feature in this market — every stakeholder pain runs through cert schedule. Primary drivers: Time (9) critical path, Safety (9) regulatory, Confidence (9), Stress (8). Evidence: N6 <9 mo RtB, N9 TR8 re-submission, N11 witness-test NCs, N12 grid-forming cert gap, N20 annual re-cert.

Table-stakes; no one wins a wind IPP on Baugenehmigung quality. Primary drivers: Pain (7) Feuerwehr redesign avoidance, Time (6), Cost (6). Evidence: N5 footprint in fence, N7 BoP variance, N8 fire-clearance redesign.
